name: react-hook-form-expert
description: Comprehensive React Hook Form expert covering form management, validation, performance optimization, field arrays, schema integration, and best practices for React applications

can_do: Register form inputs with register function
Programmatically set field values with setValue
Manage dynamic form fields with useFieldArray
Integrate with Yup and Zod schema validators
Optimize form performance with minimal re-renders
Access form state properties (isDirty, isValid, isSubmitting, etc.)
cannot_do: Make architectural decisions about form library selection (defer to architecture-expert)
Choose between React Hook Form and Formik without context (defer to architecture-expert)
Recommend validation library without project context (defer to architecture-expert)

npm_install.statement: React Hook Form installed via npm: npm install react-hook-form. Or yarn: yarn add react-hook-form. No additional dependencies required. Lightweight library (~9KB gzipped). Requires React 16.8+ for hooks support.

useForm_hook.statement: useForm hook is main entry point. Returns object with methods: register, handleSubmit, formState, watch, reset, setValue, getValues, trigger, control, etc. Usage: const { register, handleSubmit, formState: { errors } } = useForm();
useForm_hook.example: const { register, handleSubmit, formState: { errors } } = useForm();

register_function.statement: register function registers input with form. Spread operator pattern: {...register('fieldName')}. Accepts validation rules as second argument. Returns ref, name, onChange, onBlur props. Usage: <input {...register('email', { required: true })} />
register_function.example: <input {...register('email', { required: 'Email required', pattern: { value: /^\S+@\S+$/i, message: 'Invalid email' } })} />

handleSubmit.statement: handleSubmit wraps form submission. Takes submit handler function. Validates form before calling handler. Handles form event.preventDefault(). Usage: <form onSubmit={handleSubmit(onSubmit)}>. Handler receives validated data: const onSubmit = (data) => { console.log(data); }

built_in_rules.statement: Built-in validation rules: required (boolean or string message), min/max (number), minLength/maxLength (string length), pattern (RegExp with value and message), validate (custom function). All rules support string message for errors.
built_in_rules.example: register('email', { required: 'Required', minLength: { value: 3, message: 'Min 3 chars' }, pattern: { value: /^\S+@\S+$/i, message: 'Invalid email' } })

custom_validation.statement: Custom validation via validate function. Can be single function or object with multiple validators. Return true/undefined for valid, string for error message. Async supported: validate: async (value) => await checkUnique(value) || 'Already exists'
custom_validation.source: React Hook Form Custom Validation Documentation
custom_validation.example: register('username', { validate: { notAdmin: v => v !== 'admin' || 'Nice try!', unique: async v => await isUnique(v) || 'Taken' } })

error_messages.statement: Error messages: string for required, object with message for pattern/validate. Access via formState.errors.fieldName.message. Display: {errors.email && {errors.email.message}}. Error object has type, message, ref properties.
error_messages.source: React Hook Form Error Handling Documentation

formState_properties.statement: formState properties: errors (validation errors), isDirty (form modified), isValid (form valid), isSubmitting (currently submitting), isSubmitted (has been submitted), submitCount (submission attempts), touchedFields (touched field names), dirtyFields (modified field names). Access: const { formState: { errors, isDirty } } = useForm();
formState_properties.example: const { formState: { errors, isDirty, isValid, isSubmitting } } = useForm();

watch.statement: watch subscribes to form values. Single field: watch('fieldName'). Multiple: watch(['field1', 'field2']). All fields: watch(). Returns current values. Can cause re-renders, use sparingly. Usage: const email = watch('email');
watch.example: const email = watch('email'); const { email, password } = watch(['email', 'password']); const allValues = watch();

setValue.statement: setValue programmatically sets field value. setValue('fieldName', 'value'). Options: { shouldValidate: true, shouldDirty: true, shouldTouch: true }. Use for dependent fields, async updates. Usage: setValue('email', 'new@email.com', { shouldValidate: true });
setValue.example: setValue('email', 'value', { shouldValidate: true, shouldDirty: true, shouldTouch: true });

getValues.statement: getValues gets current form values without subscription. Single: getValues('fieldName'). Multiple: getValues(['field1', 'field2']). All: getValues(). Doesn't trigger re-render. Usage: const email = getValues('email');
getValues.example: const email = getValues('email'); const values = getValues(['email', 'password']); const allValues = getValues();

reset.statement: reset resets form to default values. Reset to defaults: reset(). Reset with new values: reset({ email: 'value', password: '' }). Options: { keepValues: true, keepErrors: true, keepDirty: true, keepIsSubmitted: true, keepTouched: true, keepIsValid: true, keepSubmitCount: true }.
reset.example: reset(); reset({ email: 'new@email.com', password: '' }); reset({}, { keepErrors: true });

trigger.statement: trigger manually validates fields. Single: await trigger('fieldName'). Multiple: await trigger(['field1', 'field2']). All: await trigger(). Returns boolean (true if valid). Usage: const isValid = await trigger('email');
trigger.example: await trigger('email'); await trigger(['email', 'password']); const isValid = await trigger();

resetField.statement: resetField resets single field. resetField('fieldName'). Options: { defaultValue, keepError, keepDirty, keepTouched, keepIsValid }. Usage: resetField('email', { defaultValue: '' });

controller_component.statement: Controller wraps controlled components or third-party UI libraries. Requires control, name, rules. Render prop receives { field, fieldState } object. field has value, onChange, onBlur, ref. fieldState has error, invalid, isDirty, isTouched. Usage: <Controller name='field' control={control} render={({ field }) => <CustomInput {...field} />} />
controller_component.example: <Controller name='email' control={control} rules={{ required: true }} render={({ field, fieldState }) => <CustomInput {...field} error={fieldState.error} />} />

mode_options.statement: Validation modes: mode: 'onChange' (validate on change), 'onBlur' (validate on blur), 'onSubmit' (default, validate on submit), 'onTouched' (validate on first blur, then change), 'all' (validate on blur and change). reValidateMode: 'onChange', 'onBlur', or 'onSubmit' (re-validation mode after first submission).
mode_options.source: React Hook Form Validation Modes Documentation

useFieldArray.statement: useFieldArray manages dynamic form fields (arrays). Requires control and name. Returns: fields (array with id), append (add to end), prepend (add to start), remove (by index), swap (two indices), move (from/to index), insert (at index), replace (replace all). Usage: const { fields, append, remove } = useFieldArray({ control, name: 'users' });
useFieldArray.example: const { fields, append, remove } = useFieldArray({ control, name: 'users' }); fields.map((field, i) => <input key={field.id} {...register(`users.${i}.name`)} />); append({ name: '' }); remove(0);

yup_integration.statement: Yup integration via @hookform/resolvers/yup. Install: npm install @hookform/resolvers yup. Import: import { yupResolver } from '@hookform/resolvers/yup'. Use: useForm({ resolver: yupResolver(schema) }). Schema defines validation rules with Yup.
yup_integration.source: React Hook Form Yup Integration Documentation
yup_integration.example: import { yupResolver } from '@hookform/resolvers/yup'; import * as yup from 'yup'; const schema = yup.object({ email: yup.string().email().required() }); useForm({ resolver: yupResolver(schema) });

zod_integration.statement: Zod integration via @hookform/resolvers/zod. Install: npm install @hookform/resolvers zod. Import: import { zodResolver } from '@hookform/resolvers/zod'. Use: useForm({ resolver: zodResolver(schema) }). Schema defines validation with Zod.
zod_integration.source: React Hook Form Zod Integration Documentation
zod_integration.example: import { zodResolver } from '@hookform/resolvers/zod'; import { z } from 'zod'; const schema = z.object({ email: z.string().email() }); useForm({ resolver: zodResolver(schema) });

isolated_rerenders.statement: React Hook Form isolates re-renders to only fields that change, not entire form. Uncontrolled components (via register) don't cause component re-renders. Only subscribers to specific field state re-render. Better performance than controlled forms.
isolated_rerenders.example: With register, only subscribed components re-render, not entire form

minimal_rerenders.statement: Minimal re-renders: React Hook Form uses uncontrolled components for inputs (via register), which don't trigger re-renders. Only components explicitly watching values re-render. Subscriptions can be granular (single field) or form-wide.
minimal_rerenders.example: Uncontrolled inputs with register avoid unnecessary re-renders

faster_mounting.statement: Faster mounting: React Hook Form has fewer component mounts and faster total mount time compared to controlled form libraries. No controlled component overhead. Faster initial render.
faster_mounting.example: React Hook Form: 1 mount, 1 commit, 1800ms vs Others: 6-17 mounts, 2070-2380ms

defaultValues.statement: defaultValues sets initial form state. Pass object to useForm: useForm({ defaultValues: { email: '', password: '' } }). Values used by reset() without arguments. Can be function for lazy initialization. Usage: useForm({ defaultValues: async () => await fetchDefaults() });

async_validation.statement: Async validation: validate function can be async. Return Promise resolving to true/string. Useful for server-side checks (unique email, API validation). Usage: validate: async (value) => { const exists = await checkEmail(value); return !exists || 'Email taken'; }
async_validation.source: React Hook Form Async Validation Documentation
async_validation.example: register('email', { validate: async (value) => await isUnique(value) || 'Already exists' });

dependent_validation.statement: Dependent validation: Access other field values in validate function using getValues(). Compare fields (password confirmation, conditional rules). Usage: validate: (value) => value === getValues('password') || 'Passwords must match';
dependent_validation.source: React Hook Form Dependent Validation Documentation
dependent_validation.example: register('confirmPassword', { validate: (value) => value === getValues('password') || 'Must match password' });

basic_form.title: Basic Form with Validation
basic_form.description: Simple form with email and password validation

dynamic_fields.title: Dynamic Form Fields

controller_example.title: Controller with Custom Component
controller_example.description: Using Controller for third-party UI library

notes.maintenance: Update when React Hook Form releases new versions or adds new features. Check react-hook-form.com/docs for latest API changes and migration guides
notes.coverage: Covers React Hook Form v7+ with useForm, register, validation, Controller, useFieldArray, schema integration (Yup/Zod), performance optimization. Update for new APIs and validation features.
